items.evidence: LoadingScreen.tsx renders TeamFrame wordmark + blue logo mark on dark background

items.check: Loading screen communicates progress with messages
items.evidence: Three-step message sequence with animated progress bar (800/900/600ms steps)

items.check: First screen has one clear primary action when empty
items.evidence: EmptyOrgGuide renders single 'Create first position' CTA button — no competing actions

items.check: Empty state communicates product value, not just absence of data
items.evidence: Copy: 'TeamFrame will help you map your team structure, track assignments, and keep compliance up to date.'
AppShell.tsx uses Lucide icons (GitBranch, CheckSquare, Users, ShieldCheck, LayoutTemplate, Settings) per nav item

items.check: Status bar reads as product value, not debug output
items.evidence: Health badge pills show '4 positions · 2 filled · 1 open actions · 0 needs attention' in styled chips, not raw text labels

items.check: First value moment is visually confirmed
items.evidence: OrgReadyBanner.tsx fires bottom toast: 'Your org chart is live.' with 'Assign person' CTA after first position created
OrgReadyBanner updates to 'Position filled — org chart is live.' with green checkmark icon on first filled position

items.check: Side panel empty state guides next action
items.evidence: Panel shows context-aware text: 'Create your first position to get started.' vs 'Click any node in the chart to manage it.' depending on positions.length

items.check: Org chart section header communicates value state, not generic title
items.evidence: Title switches from 'Build your org chart' (empty) to 'Org Chart — X positions · Y filled' (populated)

items.check: Active nav item has clear visual affordance beyond color alone
items.evidence: AppShell uses inset left border (blue, 1px) + background + color change for active state

items.check: Demo mode is clearly surfaced without polluting main UI
items.evidence: Demo mode badge moved to sidebar bottom — isolated from content area, amber color with star icon

items.check: Error messages are surfaced without mixing with status metrics
items.evidence: AppShell header separates health badges (left) from status/error message (right)

items.check: Brand identity visible from first render
items.evidence: Wordmark 'TeamFrame' + blue logo in sidebar header — present on every screen after load

items.check: No competing primary actions on any single screen
items.evidence: Empty state has one primary CTA. Side panel has one primary CTA per tab. Banner has one action button.
